checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture defines the canonical-data, generator, native-module, guard, human-copy, and CI boundaries; its flow diagram describes their interactions, while ## Components and ## Layer Specifications identify the existing integrations and Python, Node, PowerShell, POSIX shell, JSON, and GitHub Actions technology choices.

The directed dependencies in design.md ## Architecture run from canonical data to generator to native modules to guards, from generator check mode to CI, and from the human-copy manifest/runner to protected live files. No direct or transitive dependency returns to an upstream component.

design.md ## Data Plan explicitly identifies guard-invariants.json as the static data entity, the duplicated guard arrays and regex sources as existing data affected, and a generated-module replacement strategy with the rationale that no stored user-data migration exists; it also specifies the complete schema and validation constraints.

design.md ## API / Contract Plan specifies internal helper and CLI contracts but never states whether network endpoints, RPCs, or events are absent and never identifies whether any existing endpoints are deprecated or breaking-changed; frontend-spec.md ## API Client Strategy only says that no browser API client is added. Consequently, a downstream implementer cannot deterministically distinguish an intentionally API-free feature from an omitted compatibility analysis. This is blocking at Major because the API-COVERAGE contract explicitly requires either endpoint compatibility coverage or an explicit no-API-change statement.

design.md ## Security Boundaries names the shell-to-guard, token-to-verifier, canonical-data-to-live-guard, and lite-request-to-track boundaries and supplies authentication/authorization mechanisms, data classifications, and OWASP concerns; security-spec.md further maps each boundary to controls and acceptance evidence.

SKIP: Feature Type is enforcement-chain security and workflow hardening, and ux-spec.md plus frontend-spec.md explicitly establish that no rendered frontend, browser API client, frontend state, or backend service surface exists.

design.md ## Test Strategy covers unit-level comparator, evidence-path, risk-policy, and generator behavior; cross-runtime and human-copy integration boundaries; TEST-001 through TEST-013 acceptance verification; and concrete evidence including golden fixtures, constant-parity.tests.sh, focused suites, CI --check, static API-contract tests, isolated failure injection, digest assertions, and rollback verification.

Every component referenced by design.md ## Architecture and ## API / Contract Plan is defined in ## Components, the native-module loading contract, the human-copy integrity contract, or the requirements context; no undefined frontend or backend component is referenced.

design.md ## Components marks the risk-upgrade checker, canonical-invariants generator, and Windows human-copy runner as new, while ## Architecture and ## Human-copy integrity contract introduce build-time native-module generation plus a root-handle-relative NtCreateFile/FileRenameInfo publication pattern. Nevertheless, ## ADR Change Log lists no ADR and characterizes only the code-generation choice as scoped. These are new security-critical technology and integration decisions with no corresponding ADR, so future implementations lack a durable record of their constraints and rejected alternatives. This is blocking at Major under the ADR-PRESENT contract.
PASS under the skip condition: design.md ## Design System Compliance records N/A - ds_profile: none, and ux-spec.md confirms that no rendered components or design tokens are involved.

PASS under the skip condition: requirements.md names no bounded context or domain aggregate, and design.md ## Data Plan limits the feature data surface to static guard configuration rather than domain-model entities.
